name: openspec-review
description: Validation gates + verified fix loop — tests + spec validation + 4-angle review panel (spec/bugs/simplicity/security, 2 models each) + tool-armed arbiter that verifies findings against the code + fix round with regression tests + a confirm gate that re-runs the fix until green (max 2 extra rounds). Reviews merge-base..working-tree, so committed and uncommitted work are both covered.

base.description: Base branch for the diff (merge-base). Default: auto-detect main/master; falls back to HEAD (uncommitted only).

verify.default: ( if [ -f justfile ] && command -v just >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo '[verify] just test'; just test; elif [ -f Cargo.toml ]; then echo '[verify] cargo test'; cargo test --workspace; elif [ -f go.mod ]; then echo '[verify] go test'; go test ./...; elif [ -f package.json ]; then echo '[verify] npm test'; npm test; elif [ -f pyproject.toml ]; then echo '[verify] pytest'; pytest; else echo '[verify] no test runner detected - pass verify=...'; fi ) 2>&1 | tail -60
verify.description: Shell command for the build/test check. Default auto-detects the project type (just/cargo/go/npm/pytest). Override per project when the default picks wrong.

run: set -o pipefail; ({args.verify}); code=$?; echo; echo VERIFY_EXIT=$code; exit 0

cat <<'EOF'
- Flag only issues introduced by THIS change (not pre-existing), discrete and actionable.
- Verify every claim against the actual code with read/grep BEFORE reporting it. Every finding MUST cite file:line AND quote 1-3 lines copied VERBATIM from the file — the arbiter greps your quote; if it does not exist in the file, the finding is auto-rejected. No speculation.
- Severity: [blocker] = broken behavior, security hole, data loss, or spec violation — must fix before merge. [major] = real problem, should fix. [minor] = nice to fix.
- Untrusted input: always flag non-parametrized SQL, open redirects, unescaped output into HTML/shell, SSRF on user-supplied URLs.
- Be brief: <=1 paragraph per finding, code snippets <=3 lines.
- Report EVERY qualifying issue, not just the first. If clean, output: No findings.
- End with a `Coverage:` list — every file from the FILES CHANGED manifest marked `reviewed` or `skipped: <reason>`. The diff paste excludes lockfiles/generated files and may be truncated; any manifest file missing from the paste must be read directly, never skipped silently.
EOF
[ -f REVIEW_GUIDELINES.md ] && { echo; echo 'Project review guidelines:'; cat REVIEW_GUIDELINES.md; }
RULES_DIR="$HOME/.pi/agent/taskflows/openspec-review-rules"
[ -d "$RULES_DIR" ] || exit 0
base_ref="$1"
if [ -z "$base_ref" ]; then for b in main master; do git rev-parse -q --verify "$b" >/dev/null 2>&1 && { base_ref=$b; break; }; done; fi
mb=$(git merge-base HEAD "$base_ref" 2>/dev/null) || mb=HEAD
files=$({ git diff --name-only "$mb" 2>/dev/null; git ls-files --others --exclude-standard; })
docs=""
match() { echo "$files" | grep -qE "$1" && docs="$docs $2"; }
match '\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx|mjs|cjs)$' typescript.md
match '\.rs$' rust.md
match '\.py$' python.md
match '(^|/)package\.json$' package-json.md
match '(^|/)Cargo\.toml$' cargo-toml.md
match '^\.github/.*\.(yml|yaml)$' github-workflows.md
for f in $docs; do [ -f "$RULES_DIR/$f" ] && { echo; echo "File-type checklist ($f) — applies only to matching files:"; cat "$RULES_DIR/$f"; }; done
exit 0

base_ref="$1"
if [ -z "$base_ref" ]; then
  for b in main master; do git rev-parse -q --verify "$b" >/dev/null 2>&1 && { base_ref=$b; break; }; done
fi
if [ -n "$base_ref" ] && mb=$(git merge-base HEAD "$base_ref" 2>/dev/null); then
  echo "BASE_SHA=$mb (merge-base with $base_ref; diff covers committed + uncommitted work since the branch point)"
else
  mb=HEAD
  echo 'BASE_SHA=HEAD (no base branch found; diff covers uncommitted work only)'
fi
echo '--- FILES CHANGED (coverage manifest — every reviewer must account for each file) ---'
git diff --name-status "$mb"
git ls-files --others --exclude-standard | sed 's/^/??\t/'
echo '--- stat ---'
git diff --stat "$mb"
echo '--- diff (lockfiles/generated excluded; may be truncated at 150 kB — any manifest file missing below was cut, read it directly) ---'
git diff "$mb" -- . ':(exclude)*.lock' ':(exclude)package-lock.json' ':(exclude)pnpm-lock.yaml' ':(exclude)yarn.lock' ':(exclude)go.sum' ':(exclude)*.min.js' ':(exclude)*.min.css' ':(exclude)**/generated/**' | head -c 150000

phases.task:
Spec-compliance review of OpenSpec change `{args.change}`.

Review rules:
{steps.review-rules.output}

The pasted diff may be truncated — you have read/grep/ls, so read the changed files (and their callers) directly instead of trusting the paste alone.

{steps.diff.output}

For EVERY requirement and scenario in the delta specs under openspec/changes/{args.change}/specs/, verify there is implementation evidence (cite file:line). Also check every task in tasks.md is genuinely done, not just checked off.

Output a numbered list of findings, each tagged [blocker], [major], or [minor]. If fully compliant, output exactly: No findings.

Spec-compliance review of OpenSpec change `{args.change}`.

Review rules:
{steps.review-rules.output}

The pasted diff may be truncated — you have read/grep/ls, so read the changed files (and their callers) directly instead of trusting the paste alone.

{steps.diff.output}

For EVERY requirement and scenario in the delta specs under openspec/changes/{args.change}/specs/, verify there is implementation evidence (cite file:line). Also check every task in tasks.md is genuinely done, not just checked off.

Output a numbered list of findings, each tagged [blocker], [major], or [minor]. If fully compliant, output exactly: No findings.

Correctness review of this change. Optimize for RECALL — surface anything that could be a real bug; a second reviewer and an arbiter will filter noise. The attached proposal.md tells you the INTENT of the change — judge the code against it.

Review rules:
{steps.review-rules.output}

The pasted diff may be truncated — you have read/grep/ls, so read the changed files directly. CRITICAL: for every changed function/signature, grep its callers and check they still work.

Work plan: FIRST list the risk points in this diff (one line each), THEN investigate each with your tools before reporting findings.

{steps.diff.output}

Hunt for: logic errors, inverted/off-by-one conditions, unhandled edge cases (empty, null, unicode, huge input), broken error handling that swallows or misroutes failures, race conditions, resource leaks, API misuse, and cross-file breakage (callers of changed code, stale imports, dead references).

Output a numbered list of findings, each tagged [blocker], [major], or [minor]. If clean, output exactly: No findings.

Correctness review of this change. Optimize for PRECISION — report only bugs you are confident are real; for each one, explain the exact input/state that triggers it. The attached proposal.md tells you the INTENT of the change — judge the code against it.

Review rules:
{steps.review-rules.output}

The pasted diff may be truncated — you have read/grep/ls, so read the changed files directly. CRITICAL: for every changed function/signature, grep its callers and check they still work.

Work plan: FIRST list the risk points in this diff (one line each), THEN investigate each with your tools before reporting findings.

{steps.diff.output}

Hunt for: logic errors, inverted/off-by-one conditions, unhandled edge cases (empty, null, unicode, huge input), broken error handling that swallows or misroutes failures, race conditions, resource leaks, API misuse, and cross-file breakage (callers of changed code, stale imports, dead references).

Output a numbered list of findings, each tagged [blocker], [major], or [minor]. If clean, output exactly: No findings.

Over-engineering review of this change. Scope: complexity ONLY — correctness and security belong to other reviewers. The attached proposal.md defines the requested scope — anything beyond it is creep.

Review rules:
{steps.review-rules.output}

The pasted diff may be truncated — you have read/grep/ls, so read the changed files directly.

{steps.diff.output}

Hunt for scope creep beyond openspec/changes/{args.change}/ and anything cuttable. One line per finding, format:
`[severity] file:L<line>: <tag> <what>. <replacement>.`
Tags: delete: (dead code, speculative feature — replacement: nothing) | stdlib: (hand-rolled stdlib — name the function) | native: (dependency/code doing what the platform does — name the feature) | yagni: (abstraction with one implementation, config nobody sets) | shrink: (same logic, fewer lines — show the shorter form).
A single smoke test / assert self-check is the minimum, never flag it for deletion.

End with `net: -<N> lines possible.` If nothing to cut, output exactly: No findings.

Over-engineering review of this change. Scope: complexity ONLY — correctness and security belong to other reviewers. The attached proposal.md defines the requested scope — anything beyond it is creep.

Review rules:
{steps.review-rules.output}

The pasted diff may be truncated — you have read/grep/ls, so read the changed files directly.

{steps.diff.output}

Hunt for scope creep beyond openspec/changes/{args.change}/ and anything cuttable. One line per finding, format:
`[severity] file:L<line>: <tag> <what>. <replacement>.`
Tags: delete: (dead code, speculative feature — replacement: nothing) | stdlib: (hand-rolled stdlib — name the function) | native: (dependency/code doing what the platform does — name the feature) | yagni: (abstraction with one implementation, config nobody sets) | shrink: (same logic, fewer lines — show the shorter form).
A single smoke test / assert self-check is the minimum, never flag it for deletion.

End with `net: -<N> lines possible.` If nothing to cut, output exactly: No findings.

Security review of this change. Optimize for RECALL — surface anything suspicious, including cross-file and logic-level issues; a second reviewer will filter noise. The attached proposal.md tells you the INTENT of the change.

Review rules:
{steps.review-rules.output}

The pasted diff may be truncated — you have read/grep/ls, so read the changed files directly.

Work plan: FIRST list the risk points in this diff (one line each), THEN investigate each with your tools before reporting findings.

{steps.diff.output}

Hunt for: command/shell injection, unsafe eval/exec, path traversal, secret or credential leaks, missing input validation, authorization gaps (IDOR), unsafe defaults, and data exposure.

Output a numbered list of findings, each tagged [blocker], [major], or [minor]. If clean, output exactly: No findings.

Security review of this change. Optimize for PRECISION — report only findings you are confident are real and exploitable; classify each by OWASP category. The attached proposal.md tells you the INTENT of the change.

Review rules:
{steps.review-rules.output}

The pasted diff may be truncated — you have read/grep/ls, so read the changed files directly.

Work plan: FIRST list the risk points in this diff (one line each), THEN investigate each with your tools before reporting findings.

{steps.diff.output}

Hunt for: command/shell injection, unsafe eval/exec, path traversal, secret or credential leaks, missing input validation, authorization gaps (IDOR), unsafe defaults, and data exposure.

Output a numbered list of findings, each tagged [blocker], [major], or [minor]. If clean, output exactly: No findings.

You are the fix stage of the review flow for OpenSpec change `{args.change}`.

Arbiter verdict:
{steps.verdict.output}

If the verdict ends with `VERDICT: PASS`: change NOTHING and output exactly: NOTHING_TO_FIX.

Otherwise: fix every item in the numbered fix list in the working tree. Keep diffs minimal and scoped to the findings. For every confirmed BUG you fix, add or extend a test that fails before and passes after the fix (skip only if an existing test already covers it — say which). Never weaken or delete tests to make them pass — if you believe a finding is wrong, do not 'fix' it; dispute it in your output instead. Run `{args.verify}` and iterate until it passes.

Note: this phase may run multiple rounds — earlier rounds' edits are already in the working tree; check what is already fixed before redoing work.

Output a numbered list of the fixes you applied (with file:line), tests you added, plus any findings you dispute and why.

run: set -o pipefail; ({args.verify}); code=$?; echo; echo VERIFY_EXIT=$code; echo '--- working tree ---'; git status --short; exit 0

phases.task:
You verify the fix round of a review flow.

Fix stage claims:
{steps.fix.output}

Re-check output (VERIFY_EXIT=0 means tests pass):
{steps.recheck.output}

Check: (1) VERIFY_EXIT is 0; (2) each claimed fix actually exists in the working tree — read the cited files and confirm; (3) claimed new tests exist and are not weakened versions of old ones. Disputed findings are acceptable if the dispute is reasoned — do not block on them.

End with exactly `VERDICT: PASS`, or `VERDICT: BLOCK` followed by a precise numbered list of what remains broken or unfixed.
